[[Tyronne Roberts-Davis]] (cousin) }} }} '''Tyrone Roberts''' (born 1 June 1991) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Ballina Seagulls]] in the [[Northern Rivers Regional Rugby League|NRRRL]] as a {{rlp|hb}} or {{rlp|fe}}. He previously played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]], [[Newcastle Knights]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. Roberts has played for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]] and the [[Indigenous All Stars (Rugby League)|Indigenous All Stars]]. ==Background== Roberts was born in [[Grafton, New South Wales]], [[Australia]] and is an [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]]. He played his junior football for the [[Western Suburbs Rosellas]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Roberts is a cousin of the rugby league footballer; [[Amos Roberts]], and a second cousin of the rugby league footballer; [[James Roberts (rugby league)|James Roberts]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/roberts-handling-great-expectations/tabid/10874/newsid/74073/default.aspx|title=Roberts handling great expectations|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2013-08-18|accessdate=2015-06-27}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== After being spotted playing in the Newcastle Rugby League by [[Newcastle Knights]] coach [[Rick Stone]], Roberts was signed by the Newcastle club.{{cite web|last=Keeble|first=Brett|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/451606/knights-lock-in-teenage-tyro-as-young-stars-step-up/|title=Knights lock in teenage tyro as young stars step up | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2010-02-12|access-date=2015-06-27}} From 2009 to 2011, he played for the Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/r/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150630065823/http://nycdatabase.org/players/r/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=30 June 2015|title=R|publisher=Nycdatabase.org|date=27 December 2012|accessdate=2015-06-27}}[http://www.rleague.com//db/player/r/roberts_tyrone/index.php] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120618104731/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/r/roberts_tyrone/index.php|date=18 June 2012}} ===2011=== In round 6 of the [[2011 NRL season]], Roberts made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], playing off the interchange bench in the Knights' 24–20 win at [[Newcastle International Sports Centre|Hunter Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/newcastle-knights-in-front-at-half-time/story-e6frexnr-1226040479579|title=Newcastle Knights in Front at Half Time|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|accessdate=2015-07-02}} He finished off his debut year in the [[NRL]] having played in seven matches.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtODE5LTQtLS0tLS0xNzA2MC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-03-03|accessdate=2015-06-27}} On 16 October 2011, he was 18th man for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/11-nrl-players-feature-in-junior-kangaroos/tabid/10874/newsid/65151/default.aspx|title=11 NRL players feature in Junior Kangaroos|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2011-10-06|accessdate=2015-06-27}} [[File:Tyrone Roberts.jpg|thumb|right|Roberts playing for Newcastle]] ===2012=== In round 12 of the [[2012 NRL season]], Roberts scored his first NRL career try against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], in the Knights' 24–14 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2012/round-12/newcastle-vs-gold-coast-titans/summary.html|title=NRL 2012 – Round 12|publisher=Rugby League Project|accessdate=2015-06-27}}{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/herois-effort-from-greg-bird-in-gold-coast-titans-24-14-win-against-newcastle-at-hunter-stadium/story-e6frfggf-1226367849323|title=Heroic Effort from Greg Bird in Gold Coast Titans 24-14 Win Against Newcastle|publisher=Heraldsun.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-02}} On 4 May 2012, he re-signed with the Newcastle club on a two-year contract.{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/roberts-happy-extend-hometown-stay-5775/|title=Roberts happy to extend hometown stay | NRL|publisher=Zerotackle.com|date=2012-05-04|accessdate=2015-06-27}} He scored 3 tries and kicked 40 goals from 18 appearances for the year.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTUzMC00LS0tLS0tMTcwNjAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-14|accessdate=2015-06-27}} ===2013=== Roberts played in all the club's 27 matches, scoring 6 tries and kicking 49 goals.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTYyOS00LS0tLS0tMTcwNjAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-14|accessdate=2015-06-27}} ===2014=== On 15 and 16 February, Roberts played for Newcastle in the inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]] tournament.{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-02-14|accessdate=2015-06-27|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archivedate=15 January 2016}} He scored the first ever try of the tournament in the Knights' 15–12 loss to the Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks.{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/20-moments-2014-auckland-nines/|title=20 MOMENTS FROM THE 2014 AUCKLAND NINES|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2015-01-28|accessdate=2015-06-27}} On 19 February, he re-signed with Newcastle on a two-year contract.{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/jarrod-mullen-sidelined-tyrone-roberts-resigns-20140219-32zp4.html|title=Jarrod Mullen sidelined, Tyrone Roberts re-signs|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2014-02-19|accessdate=2015-06-27}} On 4 May, he played at halfback for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]] against [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] in the [[City vs Country Origin|2014 City vs Country Origin]] match at [[Apex Oval]] in [[Dubbo]], kicking 3 goals in the 26–26 draw.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/country-origin-team-named/tabid/10874/newsid/77968/default.aspx|title=Country Origin team named|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2014-04-27|accessdate=2015-06-27}} In the Knights' last match of the season against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], he was sinned binned for 10 minutes after he got into a punch up with St. George centre [[Josh Dugan]] in Newcastle's 40–10 win.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/newcastle-knights-finish-season-on-high-with-thrashing-of-toothless-st-george-illawarra-dragons/story-fni3fbgz-1227050569706|title=Newcastle Knights season Finish Season on a High With Thrashing of Toothless St. George-Illawarra|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|accessdate=2015-07-02}} He finished the 2014 season having again played in all of the Knights' 24 matches, scoring 9 tries and kicking 20 goals.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY0NC00LS0tLS0tMTcwNjAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-02|accessdate=2015-06-27}} ===2015=== On 31 January and 1 February, Roberts played for Newcastle in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|title=KNIGHTS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2015-01-20|accessdate=2015-06-27|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130234832/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|archivedate=30 January 2016}} On 13 February, he played off the interchange bench for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in the annual [[2015 All Stars match|All Stars match]] at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/final-all-stars-teams-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/83748/default.aspx|title=Final All Stars teams announced|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2015-02-09|accessdate=2015-06-27}}{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-indigenous-all-stars-have-beaten-the-nrl-stars-20-6-at-cbus-stadium/story-fni3fbgz-1227219053165|title=The Indigenous All-Stars Have Beaten the NRL Stars 20-6|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|accessdate=2015-07-02}} In round 12 of the [[2015 NRL season]], he suffered an ankle injury against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], ending his run of 79 consecutive games going back to May 2012.{{cite web|last=Keeble|first=Brett|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3121251/gidley-into-no7-after-injury-sidelines-roberts/|title=Gidley into No.7 after injury sidelines Roberts|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2015-06-02|access-date=2015-06-27}} After being heavily criticised about his form in 2014 and 2015, Roberts fell out of favour with Newcastle{{cite web|author=Adrian Proszenko|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/gold-coast-titans-interested-in-outoffavour-newcastle-knight-tyrone-roberts-20150704-gi55go.html|title=Gold Coast Titans interested in out-of-favour Newcastle Knight Tyrone Roberts|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2015-07-04|accessdate=2015-12-20}} and signed a two-year contract with the [[Gold Coast Titans]] starting in 2016, after being released from the final year of his Knights contract.{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/tyrone-roberts-signs-with-titans-18639/|title=Tyrone Roberts signs with Titans | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|accessdate=2015-08-17}} He finished his last year with the Knights having played in 21 matches, scoring 3 tries and kicking 54 goals as the club finished last on the table for the first time since 2005.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMTcwNjAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-20|accessdate=2015-12-21}} On 15 December, he was named on the interchange bench for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] team to play against the [[NRL All Stars team|World All Stars]] on 13 February 2016.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/teams-named-for-nrl-all-stars-match/tabid/10874/newsid/91188/default.aspx|title=Teams named for NRL All Stars match|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2015-12-15|accessdate=2015-12-20}}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/09/05/panthers-hand-knights-the-wooden-spoon/|title=Panthers hand Knights the wooden spoon|publisher=National Rugby League|date=5 September 2015 }} ===2016=== In February, Roberts played for the Gold Coast in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|title=Titans unveil 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2016-01-29|accessdate=2016-02-02|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131060105/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|archivedate=31 January 2016}} On 13 February, he again played for the Indigenous All Stars against the new [[NRL All Stars team|World All Stars]], playing at halfback in his team's 8–12 loss at [[Lang Park|Suncorp Stadium]].{{cite news| work= The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney| title=World All Stars defeat Indigenous All Stars 12-8 at Suncorp Stadium| author=Chris Garry| url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/world-all-stars-defeat-indigenous-all-stars-128-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/034a63c6602597828b4b9a5ce72f986e|accessdate=14 February 2016|date=13 February 2016}} In Round 1 of the [[2016 NRL season]], he made his club debut for the Titans against his previous club the Knights, playing at five-eighth and kicking 5 goals in the Titans' 30–12 win at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/spirited-titans-overcome-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/93487/default.aspx|title=Free Bird instrumental in Titans win|publisher=National Rugby League|accessdate=2016-03-07}} Roberts was very influential in helping the Gold Coast reach the [[NRL]] Finals for the first time since 2010. ===2017=== It was announced in October 2017 that he would be joining English Super League club Warrington on a three-year deal.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41654498 |title=Warrington Wolves sign half-back from Gold Coast Titans |publisher=BBC Sport |date=17 October 2017 |accessdate=17 October 2017}} ===2018=== He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|location=London|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}} He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|work=The Guardian|location=London|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}} After only a single season at the [[Warrington Wolves]], Roberts sought and was granted a release to return to Australia for the [[2019 NRL season]] to be with his young family. He signed a two-year deal through to the end of the [[2020 NRL season]] with the Gold Coast.{{cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2018/08/09/tyrone-roberts-to-return-in-2019/ |title=Tyrone Roberts to return in 2019 |publisher=National Rugby League |date=9 August 2018 |accessdate=14 October 2018}} Roberts played in Warrington's [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against Wigan at [[Old Trafford]]. Warrington were looking to win their first premiership since 1955 but fell short for the second time in three years.{{cite web|url=https://www.express.co.uk/sport/othersport/1031099/Super-League-Grand-Final-LIVE-Wigan-Warriors-VS-Warrington-Wolves-Score-Latest-Updates/|title=Wigan Warriors beat Warrington Wolves at Old Trafford|website=Daily Express}} ===2019=== Roberts played 15 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished last on the table after a horror year on and off the field.{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-melbourne-storm-defeat-gold-coast-titans-after-scare/news-story/8b111ca8f928e7f74c956e68be0394ed/|title=Melbourne Storm defeat Gold Coast Titans after scare|publisher=News.com.au}}{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/not-good-enough-arthur-blasts-eels-despite-comfortable-win-20190816-p52hzw.html/|title=Not good enough blasts Arthur despite comfortable win|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=16 August 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/06/humiliated-and-appalled-proctors-apology-to-titans-fans/|title=Proctor's apology to Titans fans|publisher=National Rugby League|date=6 August 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-gold-coast-titans-hit-back-at-relocation-rumours/news-story/8608e4283e2b452a9886d31b4e7eec7a/|title=Gold Coast Titans hit back at relocation rumours|publisher=News.com.au}} He also played halfback for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in their 34–14 win over the Maori All Stars in the annual [[2015 All Stars match|NRL All Stars match]]. ===2020=== Roberts played only 3 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished ninth on the table. Following the conclusion of the season, Roberts was released by the Gold Coast.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/nrl-2020-nrl-contract-news-signings-transfers-tyrone-roberts-leaves-gold-coast-titans/news-story/fc243b6b9be9fa4e9cf98cd4c2e8d616/|title= Former captain Tyrone Roberts shown the door by Gold Coast Titans|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date= 12 October 2020}} ===2022=== Roberts joined the [[Brisbane Broncos]] on a train and trial contract for the pre season after leading [[Norths Devils]] to the [[Queensland Cup]] Premiership. Due to a large number of players unavailable due to testing positive for covid, Roberts made his debut for the club in Round 1 against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], in an 11–4 upset victory over last year's runners up. Roberts played his second game of the year in Round 15 filling in for injured captain and halfback [[Adam Reynolds]] in 32–20 loss against [[Melbourne Storm]].{{cite web | url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2022/round-15/melbourne-vs-brisbane/summary.html | title=NRL 2022 – Round 15 – Melbourne Storm 32 def. Brisbane Broncos 20 }} Roberts then played out the rest of 2022 with Norths in the Queensland Cup helping them win a second premiership in a row.{{cite web | url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/qld-cup-2022/grand-final/redcliffe-vs-norths/summary.html | title=Hostplus Cup – Redcliffe Dolphins 10 lost to Norths Devils 16 }} ===2023=== Roberts joined Queensland Cup side Burleigh in the 2023 season and played for the club in their Queensland Cup Grand Final loss against the [[Brisbane Tigers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-brisbane-tigers-win-queensland-cup-grand-final-corey-thompson-retirement-keano-kini-gold-coast-titans/news-story/522cc2569f2d658a353851601c2f44d4/|title=Veteran’s Billy-inspired fairytale ends drought; Titans young gun’s final stunner: Ressies Wrap|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} ===2025=== On 29 January 2025 it was reported that he had signed up for [[Ballina Seagulls]] playing in the [[Northern Rivers Regional Rugby League|NRRRL]] competition for the 2025 season{{cite web|website=Zero Tackle|date=29 Jan 2025|title=Former 150-game halfback signs with new team to continue career|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/former-150-game-halfback-signs-with-new-team-to-continue-career-218482/|author=Ethan Lee Chalk}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://warringtonwolves.com/team/ Warrington Wolves profile] *[https://www.titans.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/gold-coast-titans/tyrone-roberts/ Gold Coast Titans profile] *[http://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/playerstats/playerprofile/tabid/10898/clubid/16/playerid/1550/seasonid/45/default.aspx NRL profile] *[http://www.rugby-league.com/superleague/player-info/11325 SL profile] {{Norths Devils squad - 2021 Queensland Cup premiers}} {{Norths Devils squad - 2022 Queensland Cup premiers}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Roberts, Tyrone}} [[Category:1991 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:Australian expatriate rugby league players in England]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Ballina Seagulls players]] [[Category:Brisbane Broncos players]] [[Category:Burleigh Bears players]] [[Category:Country New South Wales Origin rugby league team players]] [[Category:Gold Coast Titans players]] [[Category:Indigenous All Stars players]] [[Category:Junior Kangaroos players]] [[Category:Newcastle Knights players]] [[Category:Norths Devils players]] [[Category:Rugby league five-eighths]] [[Category:Rugby league halfbacks]] [[Category:Rugby league hookers]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Grafton, New South Wales]] [[Category:Warrington Wolves players]] [[Category:Western Suburbs Rosellas players]]
